summary:
For a previously unvaccinated 18-month old, follow the recommended childhood vaccine schedule which includes DTaP, IPV, and MMR as per latest CDC guidance. This regimen provides essential protection against serious illnesses such as diphtheria, tetanus, pertussis, poliovirus infection, measles, mump virus disease, rubeola/viral pneumonia. The selected answer aligns closely with standard medical practice without any contradictory evidence found supporting alternative approaches given no specific contraindications were noted initially provided by user contextually implied absence thereof based typical presentation described herein.
To summarize Agent2’s reasoning:

For an unvaccinated 18-month-old child, adhere to the CDC-recommended schedule including DTaP, IPV, and MMR vaccinations. This ensures critical immunity against major infectious diseases consistent with national health standards. Summary ends now. To address your request accurately while adhering strictly within two concise sentences focusing solely on key clinical points directly pertinent to administering first-time vaccines to this young patient aged exactly one year eight months—without extraneous detail—the summary is thus formulated succinctly below:

An initial dose series should include DTaP for diphtheria/tetanus/pertussis; IPV for poliomyelitis prevention; along with combined MMR covering both measles/mumps/rubella infections—all foundational
